question.text: Business Continuity for interoperable segments means:

answer.text: If one exchange faces an outage, participants can hedge/offset positions on another exchange
Trades are cancelled if an exchange goes down
Only one exchange operates at a time

explanation.text: **The Logic:** For interoperable segments (CDS, equity derivatives), if identical products are on another exchange, participants can take offsetting positions there. NSE acts as alternative for BSE and vice versa. **The Trap:** Trading doesn't stop — the alternative venue provides continuity. **Cross-Exam Context:** Business continuity is tested in Series I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Interoperability means positions taken on one exchange can be netted against positions on another, releasing margin.
